title: Codex MCP file-write tools (mcp__*) normalize to OTHER and bypass every write-path guard
evidence: The Codex spike (.codearbiter/spikes/codex-extension-surface.md L24-29) confirms Codex exposes MCP tools named `mcp__<server>__<tool>`, and Codex sessions can add MCP servers (e.g. a filesystem server exposing write_file). ca-codex hooks.json registers pre-write.py / post-write-edit.py ONLY for matcher `apply_patch|Write|Edit` (L30, L44) and pre-bash.py only for `Bash` (L18). CodexHost.TOOL_MAP (_host.py L163-169) has no mcp entry, so `mcp__*` falls through base normalize_tool to OTHER. A file write performed through an MCP tool therefore hits NEITHER the apply_patch write gate NOR the pre-bash shell flanks — H-18 (CONTEXT.md kill-switch), H-19 (.markers gate-pass forge), H-05 (append-only audit logs), and H-11 (immutable ADRs) are all un-enforced for that write.
impact: On a Codex session configured with any MCP filesystem/write server, an agent can overwrite .codearbiter/CONTEXT.md to disable arbiter, mint .markers/security-gate-passed, or truncate an audit log with no hook firing. Mirrors the equivalent Claude gap (MCP write tools also escape the Write/Edit matchers), so it is not strictly branch-introduced, but the dual-host effort widens the surface and the cooperative-agent trust boundary (ADR-0010) should be stated to cover it explicitly for Codex.
recommendation: Decide and document the position: either (a) explicitly scope MCP write tools out under the ADR-0010 cooperative-agent trust model for BOTH hosts (as is done for shell-indirection and self-minted markers), or (b) add a broad PreToolUse registration / matcher that also covers mcp__* write-shaped tools and route their file_path through iter_file_ops' path-shaped fallback. Prefer (a) as a documented residual unless the threat model expands to untrusted agents.
acceptance_criteria: security-controls.md states whether MCP write tools are in or out of scope for the write-path guards on Codex (and Claude)
If in scope: a mcp__*-named write payload carrying a .codearbiter/ path is BLOCKed by pre-write
